    Index

    A C D E G H I M N O P S T V 
    All Classes and Interfaces|All Packages|Serialized Form

    A

    accept(Visitor) - Method in interface calculator.Expression
    accept is a method needed to implement the visitor design pattern
    accept(Visitor) - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    accept method to implement the visitor design pattern to traverse arithmetic expressions.
    accept(Visitor) - Method in class calculator.Operation
    Accept method to implement the visitor design pattern to traverse arithmetic expressions.
    addMoreParams(List) - Method in class calculator.Operation
    Add more parameters to the existing list of parameters
    args - Variable in class calculator.Operation
    The list of expressions passed as an argument to the arithmetic operation

    C

    calculator - package calculator
     
    Calculator - Class in calculator
    This class represents the core logic of a Calculator.
    Calculator() - Constructor for class calculator.Calculator
     
    countDepth() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    The depth of a number expression is always 0
    CountingVisitor - Class in visitor
    CountingVisitor is a concrete visitor that serves to count the number of operations, numbers and depth of an arithmetic expression.
    CountingVisitor() - Constructor for class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    countNbs() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    The number of numbers contained in a number expression is always 1
    countOps() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    The number of operations contained in a number expression is always 0

    D

    Divides - Class in calculator
    This class represents the arithmetic division operation "/".
    Divides(List) - Constructor for class calculator.Divides
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to divide.
    Divides(List, Notation) - Constructor for class calculator.Divides
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to divide, as well as the notation used to represent the operation.

    E

    equals(Object) - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    Two MyNumber expressions are equal if the values they contain are equal
    equals(Object) - Method in class calculator.Operation
    Two operation objects are equal if their list of arguments is equal and they correspond to the same operation.
    eval(Expression) - Method in class calculator.Calculator
    Evaluates an arithmetic expression and returns its result
    Evaluator - Class in visitor
    Evaluation is a concrete visitor that serves to compute and evaluate the results of arithmetic expressions.
    Evaluator() - Constructor for class visitor.Evaluator
     
    Expression - Interface in calculator
    Expression is an abstract class that represents arithmetic expressions.

    G

    getArgs() - Method in class calculator.Operation
    getter method to return the number of arguments of an arithmetic operation.
    getDepth() - Method in class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    getNbs() - Method in class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    getOps() - Method in class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    getResult() - Method in class visitor.Evaluator
    getter method to obtain the result of the evaluation
    getResult() - Method in class visitor.NotationVisitor
    Return the string representation of the visited expression.
    getSymbol() - Method in class calculator.Operation
     
    getValue() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    getter method to obtain the value contained in the object

    H

    hashCode() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    The method hashCode needs to be overridden it the equals method is overridden; otherwise there may be problems when you use your object in hashed collections such as HashMap, HashSet, LinkedHashSet.
    hashCode() - Method in class calculator.Operation
    The method hashCode needs to be overridden it the equals method is overridden; otherwise there may be problems when you use your object in hashed collections such as HashMap, HashSet, LinkedHashSet.

    I

    IllegalConstruction - Exception Class in calculator
    Exception that will be used when an incorrectly constructed arithmetic expression is encountered.
    IllegalConstruction() - Constructor for exception class calculator.IllegalConstruction
     
    INFIX - Enum constant in enum class calculator.Notation
    Infix notation, e.g. "1+2"

    M

    main(String[]) - Static method in class calculator.Main
    This is the main method of the application.
    Main - Class in calculator
    A very simple calculator in Java University of Mons - UMONS Software Engineering Lab Faculty of Sciences
    Main() - Constructor for class calculator.Main
     
    Minus - Class in calculator
    This class represents the arithmetic operation "-".
    Minus(List) - Constructor for class calculator.Minus
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to subtract.
    Minus(List, Notation) - Constructor for class calculator.Minus
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to subtract, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
    MyNumber - Class in calculator
    MyNumber is a concrete class that represents arithmetic numbers, which are a special kind of Expressions, just like operations are.
    MyNumber(int) - Constructor for class calculator.MyNumber
    Constructor method

    N

    neutral - Variable in class calculator.Operation
    The neutral element of the operation (e.g. 1 for *, 0 for +)
    notation - Variable in class calculator.Operation
    The notation used to render operations as strings.
    Notation - Enum Class in calculator
    Enumeration of the 3 ways to represent an arithmetic expression as a String:
    NotationVisitor - Class in visitor
    NotationVisitor is a concrete visitor that serves to convert arithmetic expressions into their string representation in a given notation.
    NotationVisitor(Notation) - Constructor for class visitor.NotationVisitor
     

    O

    op(int, int) - Method in class calculator.Divides
    The actual computation of the (binary) arithmetic division of two integers
    op(int, int) - Method in class calculator.Minus
    The actual computation of the (binary) arithmetic subtraction of two integers
    op(int, int) - Method in class calculator.Operation
    Abstract method representing the actual binary arithmetic operation to compute
    op(int, int) - Method in class calculator.Plus
    The actual computation of the (binary) arithmetic addition of two integers
    op(int, int) - Method in class calculator.Times
    The actual computation of the (binary) arithmetic multiplication of two integers
    Operation - Class in calculator
    Operation is an abstract class that represents arithmetic operations, which are a special kind of Expressions, just like numbers are.
    Operation(List) - Constructor for class calculator.Operation
    It is not allowed to construct an operation with a null list of expressions.
    Operation(List, Notation) - Constructor for class calculator.Operation
    To construct an operation with a list of expressions as arguments, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.

    P

    Plus - Class in calculator
    This class represents the arithmetic sum operation "+".
    Plus(List) - Constructor for class calculator.Plus
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to add.
    Plus(List, Notation) - Constructor for class calculator.Plus
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to add, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
    POSTFIX - Enum constant in enum class calculator.Notation
    Postfix notation, e.g. "(1,2)+" or "1 2 +"
    PREFIX - Enum constant in enum class calculator.Notation
    Prefix notation, e.g. "+(1,2)" or "+ 1 2"
    print(Expression) - Method in class calculator.Calculator
    Prints an arithmetic expression provided as input parameter.
    printExpressionDetails(Expression) - Method in class calculator.Calculator
    Prints verbose details of an arithmetic expression provided as input parameter.

    S

    symbol - Variable in class calculator.Operation
    The character used to represent the arithmetic operation (e.g. "+", "*")

    T

    Times - Class in calculator
    This class represents the arithmetic multiplication operation "*".
    Times(List) - Constructor for class calculator.Times
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to multiply.
    Times(List, Notation) - Constructor for class calculator.Times
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to multiply, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
    toString() - Method in class calculator.MyNumber
    Convert a number into a String to allow it to be printed.
    toString() - Method in class calculator.Operation
     

    V

    valueOf(String) - Static method in enum class calculator.Notation
    Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name.
    values() - Static method in enum class calculator.Notation
    Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
    visit(MyNumber) - Method in class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    visit(MyNumber) - Method in class visitor.Evaluator
    Use the visitor design pattern to visit a number.
    visit(MyNumber) - Method in class visitor.NotationVisitor
    Use the visitor design pattern to visit a number.
    visit(MyNumber) - Method in class visitor.Visitor
    The Visitor can traverse a number (a subtype of Expression)
    visit(Operation) - Method in class visitor.CountingVisitor
     
    visit(Operation) - Method in class visitor.Evaluator
    Use the visitor design pattern to visit an operation
    visit(Operation) - Method in class visitor.NotationVisitor
     
    visit(Operation) - Method in class visitor.Visitor
    The Visitor can traverse an operation (a subtype of Expression)
    visitor - package visitor
     
    Visitor - Class in visitor
    Visitor design pattern
    Visitor() - Constructor for class visitor.Visitor
